notes: Fourth validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school empowers students with character traits, academic skills, social experiences, and a love of learning so they can shape their own destinies and become world-changing problem solvers.
Official language repeatedly describes students as climbing the mountain to and through college.
Its core values are Pride, Responsibility, Integrity, Determination, and Enthusiasm.
The homepage says the school prioritizes 51 percent character and 49 percent academics.
Its founding story says Eric and Carice Sanchez are the school's co-founders.
Stephanie Rogers is listed as president and chairperson of the board.
Its mission says the school develops students into confident, responsible, and successful contributors through mentorship and a challenging curriculum that integrates academics and the fine arts.
Official materials describe it as the first charter high school in Raleigh to integrate academics with the fine and performing arts.
Its vision emphasizes a safe, inclusive, and respectful school community of authentic learners who pursue knowledge with a creative, thorough, and rigorous approach.
The school profile says students are required to complete four arts elective credits and a course in Cognitive Development.
Its board page lists Sharon Muha as chairperson of the board.
Johneka Williams is listed as Head of School.
Its mission highlights active and creative scholarly exploration that engages students, families, community, and school personnel in the learning experience.
The school says its strong core curriculum is realized through individualized, intimate, integrated, interdisciplinary, arts-based instruction.
The homepage says academics are integrated deeply with music, dance, visual art, and theater.
Its goal statement says the school is concerned with the total development of each student, including cognitive, social, emotional, and physical growth.
The board page says the board meets on the second Tuesday of each month at 5:45 p.m.
Robin Hollis is listed as Charter Director, and her profile says she has led the school since its first year of operation.
Its homepage uses the phrase "Expedition to Excellence."
Its mission says the school embraces the natural curiosity of children and empowers them to become innovative problem solvers and community builders.
Official materials describe its program as experiential project-based learning with a STEM-focused curriculum.
The school says it implements Responsive Classroom practices schoolwide as part of a student-centered social and emotional learning approach.
Its published CREW values are Community and Collaboration, Respect and Responsibility, Equity and Excellence, and Wonder and Growth.
Tammy Finch is listed as Director of Education and Curriculum, and her bio says she is the founding director who opened the school in 2014.
Its mission says the school engages students in a challenging, relevant, relationship-based education and empowers them to foster a just and sustainable world.
Its vision statement is "Empowering Learners to Improve Our World."
Official materials say the educational program is built around four pillars: Global Education, Project-Based Learning, Co-Teaching, and Responsive Classroom.
Its stated values include Innovation, Social Empowerment, Craftsmanship, Connection to Nature, Relationships, Curiosity, Responsibility, and Reflection.
The school's accolades page says it was a 2014 U.S. Department of Education Green Ribbon School and a 2023 WRAL Viewer's Choice Award winner.
Official staff pages list Deborah Brown as Director of Middle Grades and say Steven Darroch serves as board chair.
